description: Ground-truth generative parameters for the synthetic review-attempt data. Every parameter here is the KNOWN truth that the regression analysis attempts to recover. See data/DATA_DICTIONARY.md and README.md for the narrative.

star_distribution.comment: Base log-weights are J-shaped (mass at 4 and 5), typical of review platforms. Confounders shift the star a user intends to give, which is what creates confounding with completion and length.

completion_model.comment: TRUE completion process. Logit link. The star effect is a U-shape encoded as a positive coefficient on (star-3)^2, so completion is highest at 1 and 5. Confounders (mobile, experience, prompt) also drive completion AND are correlated with star, which biases the naive estimate.

length_model.comment: TRUE length process for completed reviews (word count). Negative binomial counts with log link. The star effect is an inverted-U encoded as a NEGATIVE coefficient on (star-3)^2, so reviews are shorter at the 1 and 5 extremes and longest in the middle. Mobile shortens reviews and is over-represented at the extremes, which exaggerates the raw extreme-shortness.

rdd.comment: Optional regression-discontinuity extension. A business displayed star is the true average rounded to the nearest half star. Crossing a rounding threshold bumps the displayed rating and causes a small jump in downstream weekly review-attempt volume. This injected jump is the LATE the RDD tries to recover.
